title: What Visuals Show About Recent Damage to U.S. Military Sites in the Middle East
description: Video footage and satellite images reveal new details about Iranian strikes on U.S. targets during the latest flare-up in fighting.
summary: Recent video footage and satellite images have revealed new details about Iranian strikes on U.S. targets in the Middle East, providing visual evidence of the damage. The footage and images show the extent of the attacks, which occurred during a recent flare-up in fighting.

title: House Adopts $95 Billion G.O.P. Budget for Iran War
description: Republican leaders muscled through resistance in their own ranks to approve the budget blueprint for the war and other measures, but its prospects were far dimmer in the Senate.
summary: The US House of Representatives has adopted a $95 billion budget for the war in Iran, despite resistance from some Republican leaders. The budget blueprint still faces uncertain prospects in the Senate. The move is part of the Republican party's efforts to authorize the war.

title: Houthis Claim Strikes on 2 Saudi Oil Tankers in Red Sea
description: The Saudi authorities confirmed a strike on one of the ships. These are the first claimed attacks since the Iranian-backed Yemeni group announced a blockade on Saudi Arabia.
summary: The Houthi group in Yemen has claimed responsibility for strikes on two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea, marking the first such attacks since they announced a blockade on Saudi Arabia. The Saudi authorities have confirmed a strike on one of the ships. The incident has raised concerns about regional tensions.

title: With Saudi Deal, Trump Loosens Nuclear Standards for One Close Ally
description: Nuclear experts say it throws fuel on the smoldering fire of atomic proliferation, creating a destabilizing situation that could raise the risk of nuclear war.
summary: The US has relaxed nuclear standards for Saudi Arabia, a move that nuclear experts fear could lead to atomic proliferation and increase the risk of nuclear war. This decision has sparked concerns about the destabilizing impact on global security. The move is seen as a significant shift in US nuclear policy.

title: Coveted U.S. Nuclear Deal Is a Coup for Saudi Crown Prince
description: Saudi Arabia has for years sought a deal to obtain American nuclear technology. The agreement, signed on Wednesday, could deepen U.S.-Saudi ties for decades to come.
summary: Saudi Arabia has secured a deal with the US to obtain American nuclear technology, potentially strengthening ties between the two countries for decades. The agreement was signed on Wednesday, marking a significant development in Saudi Arabia's long-standing pursuit of nuclear technology.

title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
description: The US Department of Energy says the "peaceful" co-operation agreement will give US firms "great access" to the Saudi nuclear energy programme.
summary: The US Department of Energy has signed a landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia, granting US firms access to the country's nuclear energy program for peaceful cooperation. The agreement aims to promote cooperation in the field of nuclear energy.

title: Houthis claim attack on oil tankers as US launches more strikes on Iran
description: The Houthi attacks would be the first in the Red Sea since the Iran-backed group announced a "maritime embargo" against Saudi Arabia.
summary: The Houthi group has claimed an attack on oil tankers in the Red Sea, while the US has launched more strikes on Iran. This comes after the Houthi group announced a "maritime embargo" against Saudi Arabia. The situation remains tense in the region.

title: 'What was their crime?': BBC visits Iran school where strike killed 120 children
description: Nearly five months on, grief is still raw as a teacher and a mother recall the horror of the attack in Minab.
summary: The BBC visited a school in Iran where a strike killed 120 children, nearly five months after the incident, and spoke to a teacher and a mother who witnessed the tragedy. The event is still a source of grief for the community. The details of the attack remain unclear.

title: PC Harper's widow criticises early prisoner release plan as Burnham to review scheme
description: Lissie Harper says the possible early release of prisoners is "deplorable", after reports that two of PC Harper's killers could be freed early.
summary: PC Harper's widow has expressed criticism over a plan to release prisoners early, specifically mentioning the potential release of two individuals who killed her husband. This comes as Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham is set to review the scheme.

title: Hope for thousands of MS patients as 'life-changing' drug now on NHS in England
description: Fampridine boosts nerve signals to make it easier to walk, with up to 5,000 eligible.
summary: A new drug called Fampridine has been approved for use on the NHS in England, offering hope to thousands of patients with multiple sclerosis. The medication helps boost nerve signals, making it easier for patients to walk. Approximately 5,000 patients are eligible for the treatment.

title: Guyana ferry disaster: families demand answers as around 100 feared dead
description: Officials say 76 of the roughly 179 people believed to have been aboard survived, and 53 bodies have been found so far.
summary: A ferry disaster in Guyana has resulted in the deaths of at least 53 people, with around 100 feared dead, and officials are working to determine the cause of the tragedy. The incident has left families demanding answers. Many survivors have been rescued, but the full extent of the damage is still being assessed.

title: Jailed opposition leader Ghannouchi faints in Tunisian prison due to heat
description: The incident was witnessed by the daughter of Ahmed Nejib Chebbi, another prominent political prisoner.
summary: A jailed opposition leader, Ghannouchi, fainted in a Tunisian prison due to heat, with the incident witnessed by another prominent political prisoner's daughter. The exact circumstances and Ghannouchi's current condition are not specified in the report. This incident highlights the treatment of political prisoners in Tunisian prisons.

title: Five soldiers killed in southern Thailand checkpoint attack
description: The assault in Narathiwat comes a day after a car bombing elsewhere in the province wounded a police officer.
summary: A checkpoint attack in southern Thailand resulted in the deaths of five soldiers, following a car bombing in the same province that injured a police officer the previous day. The incident highlights ongoing security concerns in the region. The situation remains tense in Narathiwat province.

title: Calls for Moroccan rapper’s release outside a Casablanca court
description: Protesters gathered outside a court in Casablanca, Morocco to demand the release of Moroccan rapper Mehdi El Youbi.
summary: Protesters gathered outside a court in Casablanca, Morocco to demand the release of Moroccan rapper Mehdi El Youbi, highlighting a growing concern for the rapper's freedom. The event suggests a strong public reaction to the rapper's situation. The exact circumstances of El Youbi's detention are not specified in the article.

title: Armed attackers kill more than 20 in northwest Nigeria
description: The attacks reflect a surge in violence as armed gangs continue raiding villages in northwest and central Nigeria.
summary: Armed attackers have killed more than 20 people in northwest Nigeria, as part of a surge in violence in the region. The attacks involve armed gangs raiding villages in northwest and central Nigeria. The situation is becoming increasingly dire.

title: Health worker monitored for Ebola in London hospital after working in DRC
description:
UK resident was evacuated from Democratic Republic of the Congo where there is an outbreak of the disease

A humanitarian worker is being monitored at a specialist hospital in London after potential exposure to the Ebola virus.

The UK resident was medically evacuated from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) where the worker had been treating patients with the disease, the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) said.

Continue reading...
summary: A UK resident who worked in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is being monitored for Ebola in a London hospital after being medically evacuated due to potential exposure to the virus. The individual was treating patients with the disease before being brought back to the UK. The UK Health Security Agency is overseeing the situation.

title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
description:
Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well

One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability.

There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to achieve the global target of zero hunger by 2030, the annual report from five UN agencies warned.

Continue reading...
summary: A new UN report found that one in three people globally cannot afford a healthy diet, with 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs. This highlights the need for urgent action on food affordability to achieve the global target of zero hunger by 2030.

title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
description:
Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent

Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials.

According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora.

Continue reading...
summary: Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe are heading to the polls for a presidential election, with incumbent Carlos Vila Nova seeking a second term as an independent candidate. The election is seen as an opportunity for the tiny African country to demonstrate its democratic credentials. Approximately 142,000 people are registered to vote.

title: ‘We are preserving a tradition’: how Ghana’s sensationalist film posters became collectible art
description:
Hand-painted works are often wildly unfaithful to the movies they portray – reinterpretations that sometimes resulted in threats, insults and even physical attacks from viewers who felt duped

Sitting on his porch in Teshie near Accra, Heavy J dipped a brush into red oil paint and dabbed it carefully on to his canvas – a flour sack – adding blood to a knife being wielded by a man. Higher on the canvas, he had started on an outline of a skull.

Heavy J was creating a poster, but not as you might have expected for a horror film. Instead, it was for the animated fairytale The Little Mermaid. The man with the knife was not a killer but the film’s kind-hearted prince, Eric. The skull was also unrelated to the story. “We add more to make people interested,” said Heavy J, whose real name is Jeaurs Affutu.

Continue reading...
summary: In Ghana, film posters are being created as works of art, often deviating from the actual movie storylines, and have become collectible items. These posters are hand-painted and can be wildly unfaithful to the movies they portray. The artists behind these posters see them as a way to preserve a tradition.

title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
description:
Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya

Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters.

The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in which a court has ordered the work to be ⁠suspended. Construction continued, however, according to US officials and satellite imagery reviewed by Reuters.

Continue reading...
summary: Seven American aid workers who were in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a facility in Kenya after the US government introduced travel restrictions. This move has sparked opposition in Kenya, where a court has ordered the work to be suspended. The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility.
